pop.description: Pop music is polished and hook-driven, optimized for broad emotional appeal and radio play.
pop.listening_context: parties, commutes, working out, casual social settings
pop.what_distinguishes: Pop prioritizes emotional accessibility. Instantly likeable without demanding attention from the listener.

lofi.description: Lo-fi hip hop blends jazz-influenced samples with hip hop beats and deliberate low-fidelity texture for a relaxed, nostalgic feel.
lofi.listening_context: studying, reading, coding, late-night focus sessions, relaxed background listening
lofi.what_distinguishes: Lofi is defined by deliberate imperfection. Slight pitch drift, tape hiss, and gentle repetition create a safe, focused listening environment.

rock.description: Rock music is driven by electric guitars, bass, and drums with an emphasis on rhythm, dynamics, and raw energy.
rock.listening_context: driving, exercising, concerts, emotional release, focused active work
rock.what_distinguishes: Rock channels emotional intensity through volume, distortion, and rhythmic drive. It rewards active listening rather than background use.

jazz.description: Jazz is an improvisational genre built on complex harmonies, syncopated rhythms, and expressive instrumental solos.
jazz.listening_context: coffee shops, dinner backgrounds, late-night relaxation, focused listening sessions
jazz.what_distinguishes: Jazz rewards repeated listening. Patterns that seem abstract on first hearing reveal layered structure over time.

ambient.description: Ambient music uses texture and atmosphere over melody or rhythm, designed to fill space without demanding attention.
ambient.listening_context: meditation, sleep preparation, background presence, focus work requiring no distraction
ambient.what_distinguishes: Ambient music deliberately avoids focal points. It is designed to be felt rather than heard — a sonic environment rather than a performance.

synthwave.description: Synthwave is a nostalgic electronic genre that emulates the synthesizer-heavy sound of 1980s film scores and pop music.
synthwave.listening_context: night drives, gaming sessions, retro-themed events, focused evening work
synthwave.what_distinguishes: Synthwave is defined by nostalgia for an imagined 1980s future. The sound is warm, analog, and cinematic rather than the sharp digital clarity of modern EDM.

indie pop.description: Indie pop combines pop songwriting structures with independent production aesthetics — more personal and less polished than mainstream pop.
indie pop.listening_context: quiet afternoons, road trips, personal playlists, emotional processing
indie pop.what_distinguishes: Indie pop trades commercial gloss for emotional intimacy. It sounds like music made by someone who really means it, produced without label pressure.

hip-hop.description: Hip-hop is built on rhythm, rhyme, and sampling culture, with spoken or sung vocals over a beat that carries the primary musical energy.
hip-hop.listening_context: workouts, commutes, hype sessions, focused creative work, social settings
hip-hop.what_distinguishes: Hip-hop treats rhythm and language as equally important instruments. The beat and the words are structurally inseparable.

r&b.description: R&B (Rhythm and Blues) blends soul, gospel, and pop with emotive vocal performances and groove-based production.
r&b.listening_context: date nights, late evenings, background for intimate settings, emotional music listening
r&b.what_distinguishes: R&B centers the voice as an emotional instrument. The groove supports the performance rather than driving it independently.

classical.description: Classical music is formal Western concert music spanning several centuries, characterized by compositional complexity and purely acoustic instrumentation.
classical.listening_context: focused study, stress relief, concert listening, background for reading or intellectual work
classical.what_distinguishes: Classical music rewards full attention. Its emotional complexity reveals itself slowly across multiple hearings, unlike pop's immediate impact.

country.description: Country music emphasizes storytelling through lyrics, acoustic instrumentation, and a connection to rural or working-class American experience.
country.listening_context: road trips, outdoor settings, social gatherings, reflective or nostalgic listening
country.what_distinguishes: Country music is primarily a lyric-first genre. The story the song tells carries more weight than the production or instrumentation.

edm.description: Electronic Dance Music is engineered for maximum dancefloor energy, with driving beats, bass drops, and euphoric buildups designed for crowd response.
edm.listening_context: clubs, music festivals, high-energy workout playlists, gaming sessions requiring peak focus
edm.what_distinguishes: EDM is architecture for the body. Every element — the build, the drop, the breakdown — is engineered to produce a physical crowd response at a specific moment.

folk.description: Folk music is acoustic, lyric-driven, and rooted in storytelling traditions, often reflecting cultural identity or personal experience.
folk.listening_context: quiet evenings, introspective sessions, acoustic settings, campfire or intimate listening
folk.what_distinguishes: Folk strips music to its essentials: voice, acoustic instrument, and story. Nothing is included that does not serve the narrative.

reggae.description: Reggae is a Jamaican genre defined by syncopated off-beat rhythms, heavy bass, and themes of social consciousness and communal joy.
reggae.listening_context: relaxing outdoors, beach and warm-weather settings, social gatherings, feel-good listening
reggae.what_distinguishes: Reggae's signature is the off-beat guitar chop — the instrument hits between the main beats, creating the characteristic sway that defines the genre.

blues.description: Blues is the emotional root of rock, jazz, and R&B, built on 12-bar chord progressions, call-and-response vocals, and raw emotional expression.
blues.listening_context: late nights, emotional processing, focused listening sessions, rainy-day atmospheres
blues.what_distinguishes: Blues is the sound of emotional honesty. It does not mask pain — it transforms it into music through repetition, bending notes, and direct vocal expression.
